turns.student: Hi, I'm here to help you. What's been going on?
turns.patient: I've been having some issues with my stomach lately. I've got crampy lower abdominal pain, mostly on the left side, and I've been going to the bathroom a lot more than usual - like 8 or 10 times a day, which is way up from what it used to be.

turns.student: That sounds really uncomfortable. I can tell this has been weighing on you.
turns.patient: It's getting to the point where it's affecting my work and daily life. I'm exhausted just thinking about it, and taking Imodium hasn't done much to help.

turns.student: I want to make sure we don't miss anything important. When these episodes happen, does your body react in any other way?
turns.patient: I get really fatigued during and after an episode, to the point where I can barely keep my eyes open at work or even do simple tasks around the apartment. Sometimes I feel like I'm running on empty, you know?

turns.student: I know it can be scary to think about, but do you notice any sweating or feeling faint during these episodes?
turns.patient: No, I don't usually get dizzy or sweaty during an episode. My biggest concern is just getting through the day without having another bowel movement, to be honest.

turns.student: Thank you for sharing that. I really appreciate your honesty. Does the discomfort stay in one place or does it spread anywhere?
turns.patient: The pain seems to stay mostly on the left side, but sometimes it can feel like it's radiating down into my groin area. It's a crampy feeling that can be pretty intense.

turns.student: It must be frightening when symptoms like these happen. Any other areas of your body affected?
turns.patient: No, nothing else really. Just the stomach and bowel issues, to be honest. I've been worried about it though - my dad has diabetes, so I'm thinking maybe there's something serious going on that I should know about?

turns.student: I'm concerned about you. Have you noticed any swelling anywhere? Even minor changes?
turns.patient: I don't think so... I mean, I've been paying attention to my body, and I haven't really noticed anything like that. But to be honest, I'm not even sure what swelling would feel like or look like in this context. Can you help me understand what you're looking for?

turns.student: Sometimes when people are going through something like this, they also feel nauseous or have stomach upset. Has that happened to you?
turns.patient: To be honest, I've felt a bit queasy sometimes after eating, but it's not always present and it doesn't really seem to be related to when I'm having diarrhea episodes specifically. I mean, if I eat something greasy or spicy, I might feel a little off for a bit, but that's about it.

turns.student: I can see this is taking a toll on you. How has your energy been? Your appetite?
turns.patient: My energy levels are pretty low due to the fatigue from the diarrhea episodes. As for my appetite, I've actually lost some weight because I haven't been eating much - it's hard to keep food down when you're worried about having another episode right after eating. And honestly, even if I do eat something, it often doesn't sit well with me and can make the symptoms worse.

turns.student: Have you had any fevers or night sweats? I know it might seem unrelated, but it helps me understand the full picture.
turns.patient: I've actually had a low-grade fever a few times per week - usually around 37.8 to 38 degrees Celsius. It's not super high, but it's definitely been there.

turns.student: Your health really matters to me as your care provider. Can you tell me about any other medical conditions you've been diagnosed with?
turns.patient: I had an appendectomy when I was 16, no complications or issues since then.

turns.student: I know medications can be a hassle. Are you taking everything as prescribed, or has anything been hard to keep up with?
turns.patient: I've actually been self-medicating with loperamide for diarrhea for about 2 weeks now, as I mentioned earlier. It hasn't really provided much relief, and I'm worried that maybe it's making things worse or masking some underlying issue.

turns.student: I don't want to pry, but understanding your lifestyle really helps me help you. How do you eat on a typical day?
turns.patient: To be honest, with my work schedule being so long and unpredictable, I usually end up grabbing something quick and easy, like takeout or leftovers from last night. Sometimes it's not even that healthy, but it's just easier to manage when you're working 60-hour weeks.

turns.student: It sounds like your life keeps you really busy. Do you get a chance to exercise or stay active?
turns.patient: Honestly, with my work schedule and all, I don't have much time for exercise or staying active. Sometimes I'll try to squeeze in a quick workout during lunch, but it's not consistent. To be honest, just making it through each day without feeling exhausted is a challenge right now.

turns.student: I hate to ask this, but it's really important — has anyone in your family had serious health problems?
turns.patient: My older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease when she was 30. That's been on my mind a lot lately, wondering if there could be something similar going on with me.

turns.student: I'm so sorry to hear that. That must have been incredibly difficult. Do you worry about your own health because of it?
turns.patient: Yes, I do worry about my own health a lot more now. Seeing my sister go through something like Crohn's disease has made me realize how unpredictable and serious gastrointestinal issues can be. It's hard not to think that maybe there's something similar going on with me, especially given our family history.

turns.student: Given everything you've been through, I want to be thorough. Do you drink alcohol? How do you cope with stress?
turns.patient: I actually started drinking a bit more than usual lately, due to work and these symptoms stressing me out. It's mostly weeknights, 2-3 beers, but over the past month I've increased it to 4-5 per night because I feel like it helps take the edge off and allows me to relax after dealing with my stomach issues and long hours at work.

turns.student: I want to make sure we're being thorough. Do you smoke or use any substances?
turns.patient: No, I don't smoke. And like I mentioned earlier, I've actually been drinking a bit more than usual lately due to stress and these symptoms, but it's not something I'm proud of and it's definitely not helping my overall situation.

turns.student: Is there anything you've been hesitant to tell me? I promise there's no judgment here.
turns.patient: I've been worried about how much my drinking has increased, but like I said, it's mostly just a coping mechanism for dealing with all this stress and discomfort. It's not something I'm proud of, but it feels like it's helping me get through some tough days. To be honest, it's also been a bit of a concern because I know my sister has struggled with her own health issues and maybe there's a link between that and my drinking as well?

turns.student: Before we wrap up, is there anything else you're worried about that you want to share?
turns.patient: To be honest, I'm really worried about what might be causing these symptoms. The thought of having something serious like Crohn's disease or even cancer is terrifying me. My sister's diagnosis has made me realize how unpredictable gastrointestinal issues can be, and it's hard not to think that there could be something similar going on with me.
